summary: The posts highlight a range of recent small UAS developments and incidents. Ukrainian forces reportedly destroyed a Russian Ka-52 helicopter near Nadiivka, Donetsk, with FPV drones also targeting the crew during evacuation. A new Russian drone, the Klin, was intercepted; it features a 5 kg payload, 120 km range, and advanced Li-AFB battery technology. Iranian-backed militias used fiber-optic FPV drones to strike the US Victory Base near Baghdad International Airport. Discussions also touched on the tactical use of FPV carriers for ground robots and the broader strategy of using mass drone strikes to degrade Russian forces, as reported by The Economist.
